Class MariaDBFilterExpressionConverter

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.filter.converter.AbstractFilterExpressionConverter
org.springframework.ai.vectorstore.mariadb.MariaDBFilterExpressionConverter
All Implemented Interfaces:
FilterExpressionConverter
public class MariaDBFilterExpressionConverter extends AbstractFilterExpressionConverter
Converts Filter.Expression into MariaDB SQL WHERE clause format using JSON_VALUE functions for metadata filtering.

Generates SQL predicates that query JSON metadata fields using MariaDB's JSON functions. For more information on MariaDB JSON functions, see: MariaDB JSON Functions

Author:
Diego Dupin

    ISO_DATE_FORMATTER, ISO_DATE_PATTERN

    • doExpression

      protected void doExpression(Filter.Expression expression, StringBuilder context)

      expression - the expression to convert
      context - the context to append the string representation to

    • doSingleValue

      protected void doSingleValue(Object value, StringBuilder context)
      Description copied from class: AbstractFilterExpressionConverter
      Convert the given single value into a string representation and append it to the context. This method handles all value types including String, Number, Boolean, Date, etc.

      For convenience, implementations can use the provided static helper methods such as AbstractFilterExpressionConverter.emitJsonValue(Object, StringBuilder) for JSON-based filters, AbstractFilterExpressionConverter.emitLuceneString(String, StringBuilder) for Lucene-based filters, or implement their own format-specific escaping logic as needed.

      Specified by:
      doSingleValue in class AbstractFilterExpressionConverter
      Parameters:
      value - the value to convert
      context - the context to append the string representation to

    • emitSqlString

      protected static void emitSqlString(String value, StringBuilder context)
      Emit a SQL-formatted string value with single quote wrapping and escaping by appending to the provided context. Used by MariaDB and MySQL for filter expressions.

      This method prevents SQL injection attacks by properly escaping all special characters and control sequences according to MariaDB/MySQL string literal rules.

      Escape sequences:

      • ' → '' (SQL standard single quote doubling)
      • \ → \\ (backslash escaping)
      • \b \f \n \r \t → Escape sequences for control characters
      • Unicode control chars (U+0000 to U+001F) → \\uXXXX format
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to format
      context - the context to append the SQL string literal to
      Since:
      2.0.0
